config.name: Joseph
config.tagline: Lights out and away we go.

driver.name: Joseph Saddington
driver.series: Collegiate Racing Series · FARAUSA · SCCA · IMSA
driver.note: One of America's youngest pro racers — license at 12.

dailyTasks.title: Joseph's Daily Grid
dailyTasks.subtitle: Three things every day move the needle: build, pitch, post. Check 'em off.

tasks.title: Build the Roblox game
tasks.desc: Ship one improvement today — a new level, a mechanic, or a bug fix. Small progress every day compounds.

tasks.title: Reach out to a sponsor
tasks.desc: Send at least one new pitch or follow-up. Aim for one warm intro or one cold ask per day.

tasks.title: Upload racing content
tasks.desc: Post today's clip or photo from the track. Hit all three channels — keep the streak alive.

tasks.title: Sim / practice laps
tasks.desc: 30 focused minutes on the rig. Pick one corner or skill and drill it to qualifying pace.

tasks.title: Driver training
tasks.desc: Neck, core, cardio. Race-fit drivers finish strong in the last stint.

tasks.title: Engage with fans
tasks.desc: Reply to comments and DMs across YouTube, Instagram and TikTok. Community is the long game.

dailyTasks._notes: Joseph's recurring daily checklist. Add/edit/remove tasks here. Each task needs a unique 'id' (used to remember checkmarks). Checkboxes save in the browser and reset every morning; clearing all of them in a day extends the 🔥 streak.

config._notes: Personalized for Joseph 'TOSHI #81' Saddington / Saddington Racing. Edit 'name' for the greeting, 'driver'/'team' for the hero badge, 'seriesOrder' for which championships show, 'dailyTasks' for his to-do list. Reload the page after editing.

standings._notes: Current championship points per series. NASCAR & IndyCar are overridden LIVE from ESPN at runtime; the values here are only their fallback. WEC, IMSA, WRC and Supercars are a hand-curated snapshot (no public live API) — edit these as the season unfolds. 'points' is the driver's current total. 'roundsDone'/'roundsTotal' drive the Season Progress bars.

calendar._notes: Upcoming rounds used for the hero + countdown cards. The dashboard counts down to the ISO datetime in 'start' (UTC). Keep a few future rounds per series; times are approximate — edit freely.

rounds.name: Sahlen's Six Hours of The Glen
XPEL Grand Prix at Road America

items.title: Missing out on Ferrari F1 drive felt like "bleeding" to Robert Kubica
items.summary: Robert Kubica revealed that racing – and winning – for AF Corse Ferrari in the World Endurance Championship helped close the deep emotional wound of his rallying accident, which interrupted his Formula 1 career.On 6 Febr…

items.title: Jack Doohan no longer cares what other people think after F1 hardship
items.summary: Former Formula 1 driver Jack Doohan has delved into his “much stronger” state of mind, one year on from his mid-season demotion from the Alpine F1 outfit. Doohan was speaking on the eve of the Le Mans 24 Hours, which he …

items.title: Hill captures emotional O'Reilly Series win at Coronado
items.summary: A thrilling last-lap pass earned Austin Hill his first career road course NASCAR O’Reilly Auto Parts Series victory in the inaugural United Rentals Driven to Serve 250 at Naval Base Coronado.

items.title: Cup Series road course king van Gisbergen reigns again with Coronado pole
items.summary: Shane van Gisbergen remains the rabbit the NASCAR Cup Series field is chasing on road and street courses, winning the pole for the inaugural event at Naval Base Coronado.

items.title: Injured Bell and his JGR team still weighing whether or not he will go the distance in San Diego
items.summary: The biggest issues for Bell, who has a fractured left wrist, are driving one-handed around the 3.4-mile course while shifting, as well as making quick decisions with the steering wheel.

items.title: Riggs wins wild debut NASCAR Truck race at San Diego
items.summary: Short on fuel and long on perseverance and good fortune, Layne Riggs won Friday’s inaugural Navy 250 NASCAR Craftsman Truck Series race at San Diego Street Course at Naval Base Coronado.

items.title: Larson on top as NASCAR takes the stage in San Diego
items.summary: Kyle Larson will go down in the NASCAR record books as the fastest driver on the first day of track time at Naval Base Coronado.

items.title: Magnussen embracing ‘memory for life’ with NASCAR ride
items.summary: Kevin Magnussen expects to make a lifetime memory with his NASCAR debut Sunday at Naval Base Coronado in San Diego. And he’ll do it at a course like nothing he has ever seen or competed on before.

items.title: ‘Odds-on favorite’ label at Coronado doesn’t sit well with van Gisbergen
items.summary: Shane van Gisbergen is expected to end Sunday’s race at Naval Base Coronado in the same place he usually does at road and street courses: victory lane. But he doesn’t appreciate being given the trophy prematurely.

items.title: Late mechanical issue costs Marcus Armstrong shot at first IndyCar win
items.summary: Marcus Armstrong lost a near-certain victory at the XPEL Grand Prix at Road America after a late-race mechanical failure forced him to retire with just four laps remaining. After starting third, the 25-year-old New Zeala…

items.title: Christian Lundgaard wins IndyCar thriller at Road America
items.summary: Arrow McLaren’s Christian Lundgaard overcame a broken front wing on the opening lap to win a chaotic XPEL Grand Prix at Road America. The Dane, who started 13th, converted the setback and was running second with four lap…

items.title: Pato O’Ward left baffled by “wonky” tires after qualifying 10th at Road America
items.summary: Pato O'Ward was left searching for answers after qualifying 10th for Sunday’s XPEL Grand Prix at Road America. The 27-year-old Mexican, who currently sits fifth in the IndyCar Series championship standings, clocked a fly…

items.title: Malukas becoming complete package at Penske
items.summary: The newest member of Team Penske continues to lead the program with stellar performances in qualifying, including his latest front-row run at Road America.

items.title: Alex Palou still “surprised” by historic IndyCar pole streak
items.summary: There is dominant, and then there is historical.Alex Palou is rapidly pushing his career into the latter category.The reigning and four-time IndyCar Series champion added yet another milestone to his legendary resume on …

items.title: Christian Lundgaard to start 13th after “struggle bus” Road America qualifying
items.summary: Christian Lundgaard will have his work cut out for him in tomorrow’s IndyCar Series round at Road America after narrowly missing out on the Fast 12 during a frustrating qualifying session. The 24-year-old Danish driver h…

items.title: Alex Palou does something no IndyCar driver has done since 1988
items.summary: Alex Palou and Chip Ganassi Racing's magical qualifying run continued as he stormed to pole for the XPEL Grand Prix at Road America.The reigning and four-time IndyCar Series champion delivered a stellar flying lap of 1m4…

items.title: Palou stretches IndyCar pole streak to five at Road America
items.summary: Alex Palou wasn’t happy with the handling of his No. 10 Chip Ganassi Racing Honda after this morning’s practice session where he placed sixth, so the call was made to revert to the chassis setup he liked from Friday and …

items.title: O’Ward tops Armstrong in second Road America practice
items.summary: Pato O’Ward and Marcus Armstrong took turns leading the second practice session at the XPEL Grand Prix. At the end of the run, it was O’Ward in the No. 5 Arrow McLaren Chevy who emerged on top with a best lap of 1m44.003…

items.title: Ferrucci, Rossi get grid penalties for Road America
items.summary: IndyCar Officiating has announced a six-position starting grid penalty for the No. 14 entry of AJ Foyt Racing Chevy driven by Santino Ferrucci, and No. 20 ECR Chevy driven by Alexander Rossi, for unapproved engine change…

items.title: Schumacher encouraged by Road America pace as Rahal calls car ‘diabolical’
items.summary: One Rahal Letterman Lanigan Racing driver was pleased to be fast for the first time on a road course, and the other was “baffled” by the absence of speed that surrounded his car.

items.title: Armstrong leads the way in opening IndyCar practice at Road America
items.summary: Chip Ganassi Racing and its technical affiliate at Meyer Shank Racing were the dominant force on Friday at Road America.

items.title: Missing out on Ferrari F1 drive felt like "bleeding" to Robert Kubica
items.summary: Robert Kubica revealed that racing – and winning – for AF Corse Ferrari in the World Endurance Championship helped close the deep emotional wound of his rallying accident, which interrupted his Formula 1 career.On 6 Febr…

items.title: Vital step made towards USA return on 2027 WRC calendar
items.summary: Rally US believes it has “made great strides” towards bringing the World Rally Championship to the USA in 2027 following the completion of a productive candidate event.The WRC has long held an ambition to return to the U…

items.title: Supercars Darwin: Anton De Pasquale takes commanding Hidden Valley victory
items.summary: Anton De Pasquale has given Chevrolet and Team 18 their second win of the 2026 Supercars season with a convincing victory in the third and final race at Hidden Valley.After starting from third on the grid, De Pasquale ma…

items.title: Supercars Darwin: 20-year-old Kai Allen wins again
items.summary: Kai Allen has taken Grove Racing and Ford to the top step of the podium at the second Supercars race at Hidden Valley in Darwin.The rising star of Supercars celebrated his 21st birthday a week early when he rose from six…

items.title: Supercars Darwin: Cam Waters takes first win of 2026 as Ford sweeps top five
items.summary: Cam Waters and Tickford Racing have put a tough mid-season behind them with a first win of the season in the first of three Supercars races at Hidden Valley in Australia’s Northern Territory.In the first-ever Friday race…

items.title: Rossi stars at Road America as Rasmussen suffers hybrid failure
items.summary: Alexander Rossi put in another remarkable performance at Road America as the ECR driver took the No. 20 Chevy from 25th and last to sixth at Sunday's XPEL Grand Prix.

items.title: Late mechanical issue costs Marcus Armstrong shot at first IndyCar win
items.summary: Marcus Armstrong lost a near-certain victory at the XPEL Grand Prix at Road America after a late-race mechanical failure forced him to retire with just four laps remaining. After starting third, the 25-year-old New Zeala…

items.title: Late engine issue robs Armstrong of first IndyCar win chance
items.summary: Marcus Armstrong came within three laps of potentially winning his first IndyCar Series race, but the New Zealander’s potential went unrewarded...

items.title: Christopher Bell gets out of car mid-race, replaced by Brent Crews in San Diego
items.summary: During the first caution of Sunday's NASCAR Cup race at Naval Base Coronado in San Diego, Christopher Bell climbed out of the No. 20 Joe Gibbs Racing Toyota.The yellow came about 13 laps into the event, for the stalled N…

items.title: Christian Lundgaard wins IndyCar thriller at Road America

items.title: Lundgaard shakes off adversity to win at Road America
items.summary: Damage your front wing and puncture a tire in Turn 1 on the first lap of the XPEL Grand Prix and win the 55-lap IndyCar Series race at Road America.That was the wild reality Christian Lundgaard created on Sunday as he ch…

items.title: Susie Wolff names drivers to watch in 2026 F1 Academy title battle
items.summary: As the 2026 F1 Academy season continues, managing director Susie Wolff has opened up about the standout drivers she believes are already establishing themselves as the leading contenders for the championship crown.The al…

items.title: Watch Pikes Peak's Race to the Clouds LIVE Now
items.summary: One run, no second chances, and one of motorsport’s greatest challenges is unfolding LIVE RIGHT NOW on RACER Network and the RACER+ App!

items.title: Deane wins Formula DRIFT Round 4 at Stafford
items.summary: James Deane claimed his first Formula DRIFT PRO win of the season and stood atop an all-Irish podium flanked by the Shanahan brothers, Jack and Conor, at Stafford Motor Speedway.

items.title: Indy NXT race winner, Foyt Racing teammate disqualified for tire violation
items.summary: Surprise news from Road America has dramatically altered the race results from Sunday's Indy NXT Race. Race winner Alessandro de Tullio has been disqualified, as his his A.J. Foyt Racing Enterprises teammate Nicholas Mon…

items.title: AMG team penalized for both stops: "Blackout" costs Engel DTM lead
items.summary: A costly mishap for Mercedes-AMG Team Winward: Both Maro Engel and teammate Jules Gounon were handed a penalty lap during Sunday's DTM race at the Lausitzring due to a breach of the pit stop regulations. In both stops, t…

items.title: Larsson, DeJong take wins at first round of RallyX Americas
items.summary: New North American rallycross series begins in familiar fashion as two-time Nitrocross champion Robin Larsson becomes its first winner in Hypercar, while Mitchell DeJong takes the Supercar class.

races.name: Cook Out Clash at Bowman Gray
races.winner: Ryan Preece won the Cook Out Clash at Bowman Gray Stadium, his first victory in the preseason non-points race. No cars dropped to the rear prior to the green flag to start the race.

races.name: America 250 Florida Duel at DAYTONA
races.winner: Joey Logano has won the America 250 Florida Duel #1 at DAYTONA, his fourth victory in the NASCAR Cup Series DAYTONA 500 Qualifying Race. Prior to the start of the race, no vehicle(s) dropped to the rear of the field under penalty.

races.name: America 250 Florida Duel at DAYTONA
races.winner: Chase Elliott has won the America 250 Florida Duel 2 at DAYTONA, his third victory in the NASCAR Cup Series DAYTONA 500 Qualifying Race. Prior to the start of the race, no vehicle(s) dropped to the rear of the field under penalty.

races.name: DAYTONA 500
races.winner: Tyler Reddick has won the DAYTONA 500 at Daytona International Speedway, his ninth victory in the NASCAR Cup Series. Prior to the start of the race, the following vehicle(s) dropped to the rear of the field under penalty: Nos. 1, 7, 17, 24 (backup cars).

races.name: Autotrader 400
races.winner: Tyler Reddick won the Autotrader 400 at EchoPark Speedway, his tenth NASCAR Cup Series victory. Prior to the start of the race, no cars dropped to the rear of the field under penalty.

races.name: DuraMAX Texas Grand Prix Powered by RelaDyne
races.winner: Tyler Reddick won the DuraMAX Texas Grand Prix Powered by RelaDyne at Circuit of the Americas, his 11th NASCAR Cup Series victory. Prior to the green flag to start the race, the following car dropped to the rear: No. 43 (unapproved adjustments).

races.name: Straight Talk Wireless 500
races.winner: Ryan Blaney has won the Straight Talk Wireless 500 at Phoenix Raceway, his 18th victory in the NASCAR Cup Series. Prior to the start of the race, the following vehicle(s) dropped to the rear of the field under penalty: #6 (Backup Car), #77 (Unapproved Adjustment), and #38 (Unapproved Adjustment during Practice/Prequalifying adjustments, car chief ejected, vehicle will start from the rear and perform a drive through penalty after taking the green flag.).

races.name: Pennzoil 400 presented by Jiffy Lube
races.winner: Denny Hamlin won the Pennzoil 400 presented by Jiffy Lube at Las Vegas Motor Speedway, his 61st NASCAR Cup Series victory. No drivers dropped to the rear prior to the green flag to start the race.

races.name: Goodyear 400
races.winner: Tyler Reddick won the seventh running of the Goodyear 400 at Darlington Raceway, his 12th victory in the NASCAR Cup Series. Prior to the start of the race, the following cars dropped to the rear of the field under penalty for the reasons indicated: No. 77 (unapproved adjustments).

races.name: Cook Out 400
races.winner: Chase Elliott won the Cook Out 400 at Martinsville Speedway, his 22nd career NASCAR Cup Series victory. No cars dropped to the rear to start the race.

races.name: Food City 500
races.winner: Ty Gibbs won the Food City 500 at Bristol Motor Speedway, his first NASCAR Cup Series victory. Prior to the green flag, the following car dropped to the rear for the reason indicated: No. 24 (unapproved adjustments).

races.name: AdventHealth 400
races.winner: Tyler Reddick won the AdventHealth 400 at Kansas Speedway, his 13th NASCAR Cup Series victory. The following vehicle(s) dropped to the rear of the field under penalty for the reason(s) indicated: Nos. 4 and 22 (unapproved adjustments).

races.name: Jack Link's 500
races.winner: Carson Hocevar won the Jack Link’s 500 at Talladega Superspeedway, his first NASCAR Cup Series victory. Prior to the start of the race, no cars dropped to the rear of the field under penalty.

races.name: Würth 400 presented by LIQUI MOLY
races.winner: Chase Elliott has won the Würth 400 presented by LIQUI MOLY at Texas Motor Speedway, his 23rd victory in the NASCAR Cup Series. Prior to the start of the race, the following vehicle(s) dropped to the rear of the field under penalty: No. 23 (Backup Car) and No. 3 (Engine).

races.name: Go Bowling at The Glen
races.winner: Shane van Gisbergen has won the Go Bowling at The Glen, his seventh career victory in the NASCAR Cup Series. Prior to the start of the race, no vehicles dropped to the rear of the field.

races.name: NASCAR All-Star Race
races.winner: Denny Hamlin has won the NASCAR All-Star Race at Dover Motor Speedway, his second victory in the NASCAR Cup Series annual exhibition event. Prior to the start of the race, the following vehicle(s) dropped to the rear of the field under penalty in Segment 1: No. 7 (Backup car), Nos. 16, 41, 51 (Unapproved adjustments).

races.name: Coca-Cola 600
races.winner: Daniel Suarez won the Coca-Cola 600 at Charlotte Motor Speedway, his third NASCAR Cup Series victory. Prior to the green flag to start the race, the following car dropped to the rear for the reason indicated: No. 48 (unapproved adjustments).

races.name: Cracker Barrel 400
races.winner: Denny Hamlin won the Cracker Barrel 400 at Nashville Superspeedway, his 62nd NASCAR Cup Series victory. Prior to the start of the race, no cars dropped to the rear of the field under penalty.

races.name: FireKeepers Casino 400
races.winner: Denny Hamlin has won the FireKeepers Casino 400 at Michigan International Speedway, his 63rd victory in the NASCAR Cup Series annual exhibition event. Prior to the start of the race, the following vehicle(s) dropped to the rear of the field under penalty: Nos. 2, 11, 20, 21, 24, 43 (Unapproved Adjustments).

races.name: Great American Getaway 400 presented by VISITPA
races.winner: Denny Hamlin has won the Great American Getaway 400 presented by VISITPA at Pocono Raceway, his 64th victory in the NASCAR Cup Series. Prior to the start of the race, the following vehicle(s) dropped to the rear of the field under penalty: No. 6, 23, 60 (Unapproved Adjustments).

sponsors.source: saddingtonracing.com (Wen Moon / HODL counts) + confirmed deals (Satoshi / Paddock)
sponsors._notes: Editable sponsorship ledger for the dashboard 'Sponsorship Secured' section. amount = tier price, count = backers in that tier. Wen Moon/HODL counts are published on saddingtonracing.com; Satoshi/Paddock confirmed by Peter. Update counts as new deals close.
